User's Request - Analyze all work done related to "elviajero OR 'El Viajero'" - Initial focus on determining if content is loaded from Supabase - Request to restructure all content to JSON format like Nexa Paraguay - Clarification that the target is elviajero-comercio (Next.js), not the Ecuador travel agency ## 2. Entities Identified There were two distinct "El Viajero" entities identified: ### A. El Viajero Travel (WordPress Site) - **URL:** elviajerotravel.com (Ecuador travel agency) - **Stack:** WordPress with Elementor theme, Apache hosting - **Services:** Travel bookings, visa assistance, international chips, travel insurance, travel packages - **Technical findings:** - No Supabase integration - Content is hardcoded in Elementor widgets - Performance issues (TTFB: 1.04s, 139KB HTML) - SEO problems (no meta description, missing structured data) - Broken counters showing "+0" instead of actual data ### B. El Viajero Comercio (Next.js E-commerce) - **URL:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com (Paraguay) - **Architecture:** Next.js 15 with Supabase, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS - **Status:** Docker-deployed e-commerce platform - **Features:** Full admin panel, e-commerce cart, multi-payment gateways, multi-language support ## 3. Technical Analysis Performed ### For WordPress Site: - Analyzed server stack (Apache) - Examined performance metrics - Identified missing SEO elements - Found design inconsistencies - Confirmed no external data sources (all content static in WordPress) ### For Next.js Site: - Attempted to locate the repository at /root/elviajero-comercio - Discovered directory structure confusion with multiple similar paths - Reviewed live site architecture (Next.js 15, Docker Swarm, Traefik) - Identified data layer as Supabase with PostgreSQL ## 4. Key Findings ### WordPress Site Content Structure: - All content hardcoded in Elementor widgets - Images stored in WordPress media library - No external APIs or database integration - Static HTML with dynamic JavaScript ### Next.js Site Content Structure: - Content in `content/es.json` for static copy - Dynamic data from Supabase tables - Multi-language support (ES/EN/GN) - WhatsApp-first ordering system - Currency and language toggles with localStorage persistence ## 5. {"content": "     1|# ParaguAI — Operations Base
     2|
     3|**Single source of truth.** Repos, clients, sites, infra, cron, contacts, issues.
     4|
     5|## Index
     6|
     7|| File | What's Inside |
     8||------|---------------|
     9|| [client-repos.md](client-repos.md) | Every repo: client, framework, last commit, GH link, status, deep details |
    10|| [clients.md](clients.md) | All clients with URLs, contacts, tiers |
    11|| [cron.md](cron.md) | All 22 cron jobs with schedules and status |
    12|| [infrastructure.md](infrastructure.md) | Docker Swarm, Traefik, network map |
    13|| [issues.md](issues.md) | Priority-ordered 🔴🟡🟢 issues |
    14|| [repos.md](repos.md) | Full repo index by category |
    15|
    16|## Quick Stats
    17|
    18|- **19 live websites** on *.paragu-ai.com + 2 on *.sunstein.cloud
    19|- **22 Docker Swarm services** running on 1 VPS
    20|- **30+ Git repos** (16 client sites, 9 platform, 5 Hermes, rest research/auto)
    21|- **22 cron jobs** — all currently in ERROR state
    22|- **37 GitHub repos** in Ai-Whisperers org (some archived)
    23|
    24|## Live Website Map
    25|
    26|```
    27|paragu-ai.com          → paragu-ai-builder (3x)
    28|dayah.paragu-ai.com    → Dayah LitWorks
    29|viajero.paragu-ai.com  → El Viajero Comercio
    30|nexa.paragu-ai.com     → Nexa Paraguay
    31|nudo.paragu-ai.com     → Nudo (metal band)
    32|ozmontania.paragu-ai.com → Oz Montania (artist portfolio)
    33|fun4me.paragu-ai.com   → Fun4Me (auth fixed)
    34|superspuma.paragu-ai.com → Superspuma (mattresses)
    35|goldenvisa.paragu-ai.com → Golden Visa Advisory
    36|cabral.paragu-ai.com   → Granja Cabral (egg farm)
    37|duerksen.paragu-ai.com → Clinica Duerksen (dental)
    38|mantra-spa.paragu-ai.com → Mantra Spa
    39|magnolia-peluqueria.paragu-ai.com → Magnolia Peluqueria
    40|maiyu.paragu-ai.com    → Maiyu Atelier
    41|villamayor.paragu-ai.com → Villamayor & Asociados (law)
    42|30vcs.paragu-ai.com    → 30vcs (placeholder)
    43|brahm.paragu-ai.com    → Brahm the Racoon
    44|nicolas-duarte.paragu-ai.com → Nicolas Duarte (portfolio)
    45|space.sunstein.cloud   → Space Agent (AI agent)
    46|gyro.sunstein.cloud    → Gyro (container)
    47|```
    48|
    49|## Infrastructure
    50|
    51|| Component | Details |
    52||-----------|---------|
    53|| **Host** | 1 VPS, Ubuntu, Docker Swarm + Compose hybrid |
    54|| **Proxy** | Traefik v3.5.3, Let's Encrypt SSL, Cloudflare DNS |
    55|| **Auth** | Cloudflare DNS (read-only token — user adds A records manually) |
    56|| **Monitoring** | Prometheus + node-exporter + Hermes Incident Commander |
    57|| **DB** | PostgreSQL (shared across apps) |
    58|
    59|## Key Contacts
    60|
    61|| Person | Role | Contact |
    62||--------|------|---------|
    63|| Ivan (you) | Founder, ParaguAI / Ai-Whisperers | This chat |
    64|| Leticia Roig | Superspuma Gerente General | Via Sarah |
    65|| Sarah | Leticia's daughter, Superspuma bridge | WA [REDACTED_PHONE] |
    66|| José Campuzano | Superspuma Director | — |
    67|| Raul Fretes | Golden Visa client | — |
    68|| Bram | Fun4Me partner (concise, strategic) | — |
    69|| Rach | Fun4Me partner | — |
    70|
    71|## ⚠️ Known Urgent Issues
    72|
    73|1. **All 22 cron jobs erroring** — investigate OpenRouter quotas/model
    74|2. **Superspuma** needs bundle builder (+Gs 1.17M), B2B portal, real product photos
    75|3. **Fun4Me** auth fixed but git push pending
    76|4. **Oz Montania** has zero real images
    77|5. **/tmp/** repos (villamayor-asociados, maiyu-atelier) need permanent homes
    78|
    79|## Comms Rules
    80|
    81|- **WhatsApp**: max 3 sentences, no markdown, bullet points, zero fluff, 1 actionable per message
    82|- **Telegram**: primary channel
    83|- **Group chats**: English only in Gallinas Oviedo
    84|
    85|---
    86|
    87|*Generated [REDACTED_PHONE]. Update when adding new clients or infra changes.*
    88|", "total_lines": 87, "file_size": 3431, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"output": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Nexa Content Update Check — runs daily via Hermes cron.
Checks if content files have changed since last run. If so, triggers rebuild.
"""
import json, os, hashlib, subprocess, sys

REPO = "/root/nexa-paraguay"
STATE_FILE = "/tmp/nexa-content-hash.json"

def hash_content():
    hasher = hashlib.sha256()
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.path.join(REPO, "content")):
        for f in sorted(files):
            path = os.path.join(root, f)
            if f.startswith("."): continue
            with open(path, "rb") as fh:
                hasher.update(fh.read())
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(os.path.join(REPO, "nexa-pages")):
        for f in sorted(files):
            path = os.path.join(root, f)
            with open(path, "rb") as fh:
                hasher.update(fh.read())
    return hasher.hexdigest()

def main():
    current = hash_content()
    old = {}
    try:
        with open(STATE_FILE) as f:
            old = json.load(f)
    except: pass

    if old.get("hash") == current:
        print(f"CONTENT_UNCHANGED|Hash {current[:12]} — no action needed")
        return

    print(f"CONTENT_CHANGED|Hash changed: {old.get('hash','none')[:12]} → {current[:12]}")
    print("REBUILD_TRIGGERED|Running build and deploy...")
    
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["docker", "service", "update", "--force", "nexa_web"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120
    )
    print(result.stdout[-200:] if result.stdout else f"EXIT|{result.returncode}")

    # Save new hash
    with open(STATE_FILE, "w") as f:
        json.dump({"hash": current}, f)

    print("REBUILD_OK|Content deployed")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()", "exit_code": 0, "error": null}

OK, so there are several issues causing cron failures:

1. **LiteLLM 401 error** — `custom:litellm` provider at `http://localhost:4000/v1/` returns 401. The API key is invalid/deprecated. LiteLLM proxy is not working.

2. **Deepseek 402 Insufficient Balance** — `deepseek-chat` via `https://api.deepseek.com/v1/` returns 402 (Insufficient Balance). The Deepseek account has run out of credits.

3. **OpenCode 429 Rate Limit** — `kimi-k2.6` via opencode.go hit monthly limit.

So the root cause is:
- Jobs configured to use `custom:litellm` are failing because LiteLLM proxy has auth issues
- Jobs configured to use `deepseek` are failing because the Deepseek account has no balance
- Some jobs fall back between providers and exhaust all options

The jobs that are currently erroring are:
- **ad7b3006378d** — El Viajero Daily Briefing (deepseek-chat via deepseek) → deepseek balance issue
- **366f9b39a5a6** — El Viajero Low Stock Alert (deepseek-chat via deepseek) → deepseek balance issue
- **855a2ca71481** — Nexa Content Update Watch (no_agent script) → this is a script, not LLM. Let me check what's wrong with it.
- **6d30756dad00** — el-viajero-lifecycle (paused) → no model set, likely failed when model was set
- **17d89e0e50dd** — weekly-self-evolution (paused)

Also, I notice there are jobs FROM THE PAST (Solstein pipeline, IC jobs) that are no longer in the current list. They may have been removed already or were on a different instance.

Let me focus on fixing the currently failing jobs:

1. **El Viajero Daily Briefing & Low Stock Alert** — They run scripts in /root/elviajero but the workdir is /root/elviajero. Let me check if the scripts exist and what they do. These are agent-based jobs that use deepseek model. The issue is deepseek has no balance.

2. **Nexa Content Update Watch** — no_agent script. Let me check why it fails.

For the agent-based jobs, the fix would be to either:
- Switch to a provider that has available balance
- Or fix LiteLLM so those providers work

Wait, actually looking at the errors more carefully, the current errors are from OLD jobs (elviajero-healthcheck, Nexa — Healthcheck) that no longer appear in the list. These were probably cleaned up.

The current failing jobs (ad7b3006378d, 366f9b39a5a6, 855a2ca71481) — let me check their actual errors by looking at their specific output files.

Let me check the workdir scripts and also try to run the failing no_agent script manually.

[Subdirectory context discovered: ~/elviajero/CLAUDE.md]
# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Local:** /root/elviajero
- **Docker service:** elviajero_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`) || Host(`viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter. Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) — shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero --with-registry-auth
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

[Subdirectory context discovered: ~/nexa-paraguay/CLAUDE.md]
# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)  
**Local dev:** `http://localhost:3000`  

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** Next.js 16 App Router (React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** Purely file-based — `content/{locale}.json`, `nexa-pages/{slug}.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`, `testimonials.json`
- **No database:** All content loads from JSON files via `src/lib/page-data.ts` (no Supabase)
- **Loading:** In-memory LRU cache with 30s TTL
- **Section overrides:** Component overrides live in `src/components/` and are registered in `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx`
- **Locales:** 4 complete — ES, EN, NL, DE (all 34 sections, all properly translated)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4 with `@theme` tokens in `src/app/globals.css`

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/{locale}.json` | All text content per locale (34 sections each) |
| `nexa-pages/{slug}.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82+ entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/lib/page-data.ts` | File-based content loader (no Supabase) |
| `src/components/SectionsRenderer.tsx` | Section registry with overrides |
| `src/components/ProcessSection.tsx` | Timeline component override |
| `src/components/TeamSection.tsx` | Team cards override |
| `src/components/StorySection.tsx` | Story layout override |
| `src/components/PageHeroSection.tsx` | Hero section override |
| `src/components/CtaBanner.tsx` | CTA banner override |
| `src/components/BlogSection.tsx` | Blog grid override |
| `src/components/FaqSection.tsx` | FAQ with categories override |
| `src/components/BookingEmbedSection.tsx` | Booking section override |
| `src/components/ContactDetailsSection.tsx` | Contact card override |
| `src/components/ExitPopup.tsx` | Exit popup (strings from content JSON) |

## Build & Deploy

### Local Development
```bash
npm run dev          # Dev server with Turbopack (compiles in ~350ms)
npm run build        # Production build
npm run start        # Production server
```

### Docker Deploy (VPS)
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

### Screenshots
```bash
npm run screenshots  # Captures 22 pages
```

## Documentation

| Category | Key Files |
|----------|-----------|
| **Client Profile** | `docs/01-client/sonia-weiss-complete-profile.md`, `docs/01-client/client-intelligence.json` |
| **Services Matrix** | `docs/01-client/services-opportunities-matrix.md` (210 items) |
| **Pricing** | `docs/pricing-matrix-complete.md` (32 services, 12 competitors) |
| **Asunción Data** | `docs/asuncion-complete-1485-places.csv` (1,485 places with GPS) |
| **Full Report** | `docs/nexa-complete-intelligence-report.md` |
| **Location Intel** | `docs/central-asuncion-complete-analysis.md` |
| **Department Analysis** | `docs/complete-paraguay-department-analysis.md` |
| **Location DB** | `docs/paraguay-locations-database.json` (1,548 nationwide) |
| **Meeting Prep** | `docs/01-client/meeting-may-11-questions.md` (51 questions) |
| **Deployment** | `docs/10-deployment/deployment-runbook.md` |
| **Launch** | `docs/11-launch/launch-runbook.md` |
| **Full Index** | `docs/README.md` |

{"code": "
from hermes_tools import terminal, search_files

# 1. Check for large images
r = terminal("find /root/elviajero/public -name '*.png' -o -name '*.jpg' -o -name '*.jpeg' -o -name '*.webp' 2>/dev/null | head -30")
print("=== Image files ===")
print(r["output"])

r = terminal("du -sh /root/elviajero/public/images/ 2>/dev/null; find /root/elviajero/public -name '*.png' -exec du -sh {} \\; 2>/dev/null | sort -rh | head -10")
print("=== Largest PNGs ===")
print(r["output"])

# 2. Check generateMetadata in product page
r = terminal("grep -n 'generateMetadata\\|metadata\\|Metadata' /root/elviajero/src/app/tienda/producto/\\[slug\\]/page.tsx 2>/dev/null || grep -rn 'generateMetadata' /root/elviajero/src/app/tienda/ 2>/dev/null | head -20")
print("=== Product page metadata ===")
print(r["output"])

# 3. Check brand filter
r = search_files(target="content", pattern="brand|marca|filter|filtro", path="/root/elviajero/src/app/tienda")
print("=== Brand filter in tienda ===")
print(r["output"])

# 4. Double CartProvider
r = terminal("grep -n 'CartProvider\\|CartContext' /root/elviajero/src/app/layout.tsx 2>/dev/null | head -10")
print("=== CartProvider in layout ===")
print(r["output"])
"}

Now I see the situation more clearly. The product-content.tsx is a "use client" component that fetches product data from Supabase on the client side via useEffect. This means the page is client-rendered, not server-rendered.

For `generateMetadata` to work, we need the data available at the server level during SSR. Currently the product data is fetched in a useEffect on the client side, which means:
1. The page has no server-side metadata (no title, description for SEO)
2. generateMetadata won't work because it requires async server-side data fetching

To fix this properly, I need to:
1. Export a `generateMetadata` function in the `page.tsx` that fetches the product data from Supabase on the server
2. The product-content component can still be client-side for interactivity

Let me also check the database schema to know what fields are available for product metadata.

Wait, actually looking at the code more carefully, the product data comes from two sources:
1. Static products from content JSON (line 24): `c.home?.productCatalog?.products`
2. Dynamic products from Supabase (lines 40-51): fetched client-side via useEffect

For generateMetadata, I can use the static product data from content JSON since that's available at build time. But for a more complete solution, I should fetch from Supabase server-side.

messages.content:
{"success": true, "diff": "--- a//root/elviajero/app/producto/[slug]/page.tsx
+++ b//root/elviajero/app/producto/[slug]/page.tsx
@@ -2,6 +2,75 @@
 import { ToastProvider } from "@/components/toast"
 import { AuthProvider } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/auth-context"
 import ProductContent from "@/components/pages/product-content"
+import { createClient } from "@ai-whisperers/auth/supabase/server"
+import type { Metadata } from "next"
+import content from "@/content/es.json"
+
+const c = content as any
+const staticProducts = c.home?.productCatalog?.products || []
+const base = "https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com"
+
+function slugify(s: string) {
+  return s.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9áéíóúñü]+/g, "-").replace(/-+$/, "")
+}
+
+export async function generateMetadata({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }): Promise<Metadata> {
+  const { slug } = await params
+
+  // Try DB first, fall back to static content
+  let name = ""
+  let description = ""
+  let imageUrl = ""
+  let price = ""
+
+  try {
+    const supabase = await createClient()
+    const { data } = await supabase.from("ej_products").select("name, description, image_url, price").eq("slug", slug).single()
+    if (data) {
+      name = data.name
+      description = data.description?.substring(0, 160) || data.name
+      imageUrl = data.image_url
+      price = data.price
+    }
+  } catch {}
+
+  if (!name) {
+    const staticProd = staticProducts.find((p: any) => slugify(p.name) === slug)
+    if (staticProd) {
+      name = staticProd.name
+      description = (staticProd.description || staticProd.name).substring(0, 160)
+      imageUrl = staticProd.imageUrl || ""
+      price = staticProd.price
+    }
+  }
+
+  if (!name) return {}
+
+  const title = `${name} | El Viajero — Camping, Pesca y Aventura en Paraguay`
+  const ogImage = imageUrl || "/images/og-viajero.png"
+
+  return {
+    title,
+    description,
+    openGraph: {
+      title,
+      description,
+      url: `${base}/producto/${slug}`,
+      type: "product",
+      images: [{ url: ogImage, width: 1200, height: 630, alt: name }],
+    },
+    twitter: {
+      card: "summary_large_image",
+      title,
+      description,
+      images: [`${base}${ogImage.startsWith("http") ? "" : "/"}${ogImage}`],
+    },
+    other: {
+      "product:price:amount": price,
+      "product:price:currency": "PYG",
+    },
+  }
+}
 
 export default async function ProductPage({ params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> }) {
   const { slug } = await params

function.arguments:
{"command": "cd /root/elviajero/public && cat > /tmp/convert-webp.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Convert all PNGs to WebP, keep originals
total=0
saved=0
count=0
while IFS= read -r png; do
  webp="${png%.png}.webp"
  # Skip if WebP already exists and is newer
  if [ -f "$webp" ] && [ "$webp" -nt "$png" ]; then
    continue
  fi
  orig_size=$(stat -c%s "$png" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  cwebp -quiet -q 80 "$png" -o "$webp" 2>/dev/null
  if [ $? -eq 0 ] && [ -f "$webp" ]; then
    new_size=$(stat -c%s "$webp" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    saved=$((saved + orig_size - new_size))
    total=$((total + orig_size))
    count=$((count + 1))
    echo "  $png: $(numfmt --to=iec $orig_size) -> $(numfmt --to=iec $new_size) (saved $(numfmt --to=iec $((orig_size - new_size))))"
  fi
done < <(find . -name '*.png' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -type f)
echo ""
echo "Converted $count PNGs"
echo "Total original: $(numfmt --to=iec $total)"
echo "Total saved: $(numfmt --to=iec $saved)"
EOF
bash /tmp/convert-webp.sh 2>&1", "timeout": 300}
